---
output: 
  html_document:
    css: style.css
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    toc_float: true
encoding: UTF-8
---

<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Montserrat:wght@400;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">

<!-- Main Banner Image -->
<div style="width: 100%;
            background-image: url('title_banner.png'); 
            background-size: contain;
            background-repeat: no-repeat;
            background-position: center;
            padding: 75px 10px 80px 30px;
            color: white;
            font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif;
            text-align: left;">
  <div style="font-size: 32px; margin: 0; letter-spacing: 2px;">Retail Sales Dashboard</div>
  <p style="margin: 0; color: white !important; letter-spacing: 1px;">Fictional Data — CSS Style Test</p>
</div>

<!-- Toggle Button Script -->
<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
  document.querySelectorAll(".toggleButton").forEach(function(button) {
    let originalText = button.innerText;
    button.addEventListener("click", function() {
      var targetId = this.getAttribute("data-target");
      var content = document.getElementById(targetId);
      if (content) {
        content.style.display = (content.style.display === "none") ? "block" : "none";
        this.innerText = content.style.display === "none" ? originalText : "Hide " + originalText.substring(5);
      }
    });
  });
});
</script>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo       = FALSE,
  warning    = FALSE,
  message    = FALSE
)
```

```{r generate_data, include=FALSE}
# ── Sales summary metrics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
total_revenue    <- 4823650
total_orders     <- 18342
avg_order_value  <- round(total_revenue / total_orders, 2)
return_rate      <- 4.7
top_region       <- "New South Wales"

# ── Sales by region ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sales_by_region <- tibble(
  Region    = c("New South Wales", "Victoria", "Queensland", 
                "Western Australia", "South Australia"),
  Orders    = c(5821, 4932, 3847, 2401, 1341),
  Revenue   = c(1542300, 1298700, 987400, 634100, 361150),
  Avg_Order = round(Revenue / Orders, 2),
  Growth    = c("↑ 12.4%", "↑ 8.1%", "↑ 5.3%", "↓ 2.1%", "↑ 1.8%")
)

# ── Sales by category ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sales_by_category <- tibble(
  Category  = c("Electronics", "Apparel", "Home & Garden", 
                "Sports", "Beauty"),
  Orders    = c(4821, 5103, 3642, 2987, 1789),
  Revenue   = c(1823400, 987600, 743200, 821300, 448150),
  Top_Product = c("Wireless Earbuds", "Running Shoes", 
                  "Garden Tool Set", "Yoga Mat", "Moisturiser SPF50")
)

# ── Monthly revenue ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
monthly_revenue <- tibble(
  Month   = month.abb[1:6],
  Revenue = c(712400, 698200, 821300, 743600, 912800, 935350),
  Orders  = c(2841, 2763, 3124, 2987, 3412, 3215)
)
```

---

## Executive Summary

<div class="summary-box">
Total revenue for the first half of FY2025 reached **$4.82M** across **18,342 orders**. New South Wales remains the top performing region, contributing 32% of total revenue. June was the strongest month on record with $935K in revenue.
</div>

---

## Key Metrics

<div class="metric-grid">
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">`r paste0("$", formatC(total_revenue, format="f", digits=0, big.mark=","))`</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Total Revenue</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">`r formatC(total_orders, format="f", digits=0, big.mark=",")`</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Total Orders</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">`r paste0("$", avg_order_value)`</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Average Order Value</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value" style="color: #2ca02c;">`r paste0(return_rate, "%")`</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Return Rate</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">`r top_region`</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Top Performing Region</div>
  </div>
</div>

---

## Sales by Region {.tabset}

### Overview

```{r region_table}
create_region_table <- function(df, header_color = "#3ba5ae") {
  df %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "html",
      align     = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
      escape    = FALSE,
      col.names = c("Region", "Orders", "Revenue ($)", 
                    "Avg Order ($)", "YoY Growth"),
      format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      full_width        = TRUE,
      bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "bordered", "hover", "striped"),
      position          = "center",
      font_size         = 12
    ) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = header_color, color = "white") %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "200px") %>%
    column_spec(2, width = "100px") %>%
    column_spec(3, width = "120px") %>%
    column_spec(4, width = "120px") %>%
    column_spec(5, width = "100px")
}

create_region_table(sales_by_region)
```

### Insight

<div class="info-box">
Western Australia is the only region showing negative year-on-year growth at -2.1%. This may warrant further investigation into local market conditions or fulfilment issues.
</div>

<div class="warning-box">
South Australia order volumes are low relative to population size. Consider targeted promotional activity in this region for H2 FY2025.
</div>

---

## Sales by Category {.tabset}

### Overview

```{r category_table}
create_category_table <- function(df, header_color = "#3ba5ae") {
  df %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "html",
      align     = c("l", "c", "c", "l"),
      escape    = FALSE,
      col.names = c("Category", "Orders", "Revenue ($)", "Top Product"),
      format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      full_width        = TRUE,
      bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "bordered", "hover", "striped"),
      position          = "center",
      font_size         = 12
    ) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = header_color, color = "white") %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "150px") %>%
    column_spec(2, width = "100px") %>%
    column_spec(3, width = "120px") %>%
    column_spec(4, width = "200px")
}

create_category_table(sales_by_category)
```

### Detail

<div style="display: flex; justify-content: center; margin-bottom: 1em;">
  <button class="toggleButton" data-target="category_detail">
    Show Category Notes
  </button>
</div>

<div id="category_detail" class="codeContent" style="display:none;">

<div class="highlight-box">
**Electronics** is the highest revenue category at $1.82M despite ranking second in order volume — driven by high average order values. Wireless Earbuds remain the top selling product for the third consecutive quarter.
</div>

<div class="info-box">
**Apparel** leads in order volume at 5,103 orders. Running Shoes account for 34% of category revenue.
</div>

</div>

---

## Monthly Revenue

```{r monthly_table}
monthly_revenue %>%
  kbl(
    format    = "html",
    align     = c("l", "c", "c"),
    escape    = FALSE,
    col.names = c("Month", "Revenue ($)", "Orders"),
    format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width        = TRUE,
    bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "bordered", "hover", "striped"),
    position          = "center",
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#3ba5ae", color = "white") %>%
  row_spec(6, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f4f5")
```

<p class="text-muted">Source: Fictional retail dataset — for style testing purposes only.</p>

---

## Notes

<div class="spacer"></div>

<div class="summary-box">
This report is a style test for the CSS HTML Styling Quick Reference Guide. All data is fictional and generated purely to demonstrate CSS elements in a realistic reporting context.
</div>
